raw_input:
[5.0/5] Too Expensive? Get the Refill Pack! I'm a sucker for beautiful packaging.
Here we have a gorgeous tea canister that is well made and beautiful. It also closes tightly, keeping my tea fresh. A good tea canister is not cheap, so I didn't have sticker shock with this product.

Thankfully, the company thoughtfully puts out refill packs of this delicious and calming tea.
I'll definitely be ordering a refill because not only do I like it myself, but after going through so many different calming and sleep inducing teas, this is my spouse's absolute favorite. Not just because of the taste, but it helps her to totally relax and even sleep throughout the night. Other teas and sleep formulations such as melatonin and valerian supplements just don't do the trick.
For me, it's more about the delightful taste. Slightly floral, slightly sweet. Yes, it does relax me and is helpful as part of a nighttime ritual, but not as much as my spouse. We both love it but for different reasons.

And I just adore the look of the canister. A lovely gloss ceramic, with an Art Deco nod, that fits in any decor. No hiding it, it's proudly on the counter, ready for anytime you need a little tea comfort. [3.0/5] Mild effectiveness This made me slightly more sleepy (using 2 bags per). Not a huge difference. My melatonin pills are still my go-to when it is time to get some shuteye. I probably won't get more when I'm done because these did not taste good at all.

The jar they came in, however, is quite awesome!! I totally intend to reuse it somehow when I'm done with this. Sadly, it is probably the best part of this. [3.0/5] The best thing about this is the jar it comes in If you look at the photo attached you’ll see that the package has a spot that reads “Don’t be put off by the pungent odour! It’s just the strength of the Valerian Root. (It disappears when in hot water).”

I believed that message. I should not have believed that message. It smelled like old gym socks left in a locker for a year and, while the taste was not as bad as the odor (or odour, if you’re Australian) it wasn’t very pleasant either.

As a food scientist/chef I always look back at my mentor telling me “It ain’t nutrition until it passes the lips”. It doesn’t matter how healthy something is, if someone won’t eat it or drink it, it can’t do them any good. Taste matters.

Taste is subjective and you may not be offended by the taste, I was.
As for its effectiveness, I can’t really say. There is literature that backs up to some extent, the claims made and it very well may help calm you or help you get to sleep. Then again, the same can be said for a cup of warm decaffeinated tea. Perhaps this is better than that but I’m not drinking it.

The packaging is very nice, it would make a nice gift for someone who is into this type of product. The painted glass jar is nice as well (the cap is plastic).

For what this costs, they should have at least used better tea bags; asking that the bags have strings w/ tabs on them, or that they are made of a higher-grade paper, shouldn’t be too much to ask when a teabag costs more than $1.00 per.

Again, the taste is subjective, you may like the flavor or, at least, not find it offensive as I did.

Review of: WelleCo - Sleep Welle Natural Calming Tea Caddy [3.0/5] Beautiful (unnecessary?) packaging, ok taste I was surprised at the packaging when this tea arrived. It's pretty elegant, especially for tea. I would expect a high-end candle to look like this, not a canister of tea! It's beautiful, but again, for tea. The box is nice with a soft, thick elastic band to keep the top on. The jar is a light purple glass with a plastic lid. They did slap a warning about the horrific smell when you open the cap. It's pretty bad and strong. However, I bravely pushed on and made a cup. It doesn't really smell that bad once it's steeped. I steep it in 195 degree water for 7 minutes. It does seem to take the edge off, but the flavor is really just okay. At $68 (currently) for 50 tea bags, I think the packaging must be really expensive. I usually drink Yogi Bedtime Tea because it's readily available at the grocery store and I love the way it smells and tastes. While I think this WelleCo tea seems to be more effective, I really would prefer to just drink a cup of nice-tasting tea before bed. [5.0/5] Sleepy tea I like the taste of the tea and it does make you feel very calm. [3.0/5] Most Of The Effort Was Put Into The Packaging The Sleep Welle Natural Calming Tea by WelleCo put a lot of effort into the presentation to reflect a high end product. The price also reflects that at $68.50 for 50 tea bags. The packaging was nice, but I didn’t think it was THAT nice. The canister is unique, but I expected something heavier and more substantial for the price.

The flavor of the tea is nice, but doesn’t seem anything particularly special. I’m having to use two tea bags for an 8 oz. cup for it to be strong enough to really taste. I had some sweetener and it is a pleasant cup of tea. I don’t feel like it helps me sleep, but I make it the afternoons for a relaxing break. But having to use two tea bags makes the pricing even more ridiculous, since I will only get 25 cups of tea from it (not even a whole month’s worth at 1 cup/day).

A word of advice: don’t give up on it when you open the container and smell it. It smelled not so pleasant to me. But I was game, and still gave it a try. It definitely smells a whole lot better than it smells! It also smells much better once it’s brewed.

Honestly, I can’t find anything to justify the cost. It does taste nice brewing it with two bags. I’m just not living the lifestyle of spending over $100 for a month’s worth of tea. [3.0/5] Don’t smell before you brew it! I’m a huge tea drinker and was looking for something different to drink before bed instead of my usual chamomile blend. I had heard of this Welle co brand before, but had never personally tried it prior to this. So I was really excited to give it a go.

First impressions, the box it comes in is super luxe looking and very giftable. And the container that holds the tea bags is a very pretty purple color and is glass, giving you that premium look. However once I opened the container I was highly disappointed. They didn’t even splurge on the pyramid tea bags, they went with those cheap pouches. For this price point I expected much better if I’m being honest!

Scent wise this tea smells like sweaty socks, it is not a pleasant smell and I highly recommend not even smelling it before brewing because it can easily put you off to drinking it. But once brewing I was surprised that the scent was almost fruity, so much better than in the container! The directions say to steep one to two bags for 2-10 minutes, in very hot but not boiling water. I usually like stronger tea, but with this I just can’t justify using 2 tea bags at that price point!

Taste wise it actually has a very mild taste, and none of that sticky socks smell or taste at all. It’s very mild tasting with a slightly fruity/berry taste to it. Is it amazing? No, but it’s not bad. It’s definitely an acquired taste! I will say though that I have been sleeping like a baby the last few nights I have drank this, so even though it’s not my favorite tea it does seam to work, at least for me. [3.0/5] Ridiculously overpriced but tastes good The caddy is heavy and gorgeous(I have a thing for packaging and will definitely reuse this). The tea itself is good, has a very fruity with a slight bitter flavor, but more of a daytime tea for me to relax and enjoy rather than a nighttime tea before bed(Anima Mundi Herbals has a Lucid Dream Tea that is way more effective to help me sleep). This didn’t help me sleep and it’s ridiculously overpriced when you can get tea with basically the same ingredients for less than $10 elsewhere. I’m giving three stars just because the caddy is gorgeous,reusable, and the tea is yummy if you like tropical fruity teas. [5.0/5] So Calming & Soothing I really love drinking this tea to calm down after a day of hard work or just before bed. This tea comes in a very nice, very well made, reusable glass container. I love how the container really holds in each tea bags flavor compared to each individually wrapped in paper packets or in a paper box. I will definitely continue to use this glass container to store all of my future teas in as well as it is so pretty! I am really in love with this calming, soothing and subtle tasting tea. I do not prefer to add sugar, honey, milk, etc to my teas. I think this tea is perfect as is and will definitely continue to drink this to calm my nerves at the end of the night. [5.0/5] So good Really helps me relax and sleep peacefully. Better than most on the market I’ve tried! [5.0/5] Great product Definitely has helped in calming at the end of the night allowing for an easier night sleep. [4.0/5] Tasty tea but needs improvement The tea is calming and very tasty if you can get over the pungent smell that comes from the tea bags before infusion. The smell is not a problem now for me but it took time to get used to it. The bottle is pricey. I would recommend just buying the tea bags and storing in a tea canister. For the price of the tea bags I would real natural ingredients instead of added flavors. I have tried a few calming teas but I find the taste of this tea to surpass all the other teas.

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

This calming sleep tea comes in a highly praised glass canister that most reviewers loved, describing it as beautiful, elegant, and reusable. However, opinions on the tea itself were more mixed.

The most common complaint was the strong, unpleasant smell of the dry tea bags, which many compared to sweaty gym socks. This was attributed to the valerian root ingredient. Most reviewers noted that the smell improves significantly once brewed, and the taste was generally described as mild, slightly fruity, and slightly floral.

Many reviewers felt the product was overpriced at around $68 for 50 tea bags, especially since several found they needed to use two bags per cup to get a strong enough brew, effectively doubling the cost per serving. Some also noted disappointment that the bags were basic pouches rather than premium pyramid bags given the price point.

In terms of effectiveness, results were mixed. Some reviewers found it genuinely helpful for relaxing and sleeping, while others felt it had only mild effects and needed to supplement with melatonin or other sleep aids.

Overall, the packaging received near-universal praise and many plan to reuse the canister. The tea itself has a pleasant enough taste once brewed, but the high price, unpleasant dry smell, and inconsistent sleep benefits left many reviewers feeling it was not worth the cost compared to more affordable alternatives.
